summary: A country shattered by a civil war (2011–present) that killed 500,000+ people and displaced half its pre-war population of 22 million, becoming the arena for a proxy conflict involving Russia, Iran, Turkey, the US, Israel, and Gulf states.

history.detail: France takes Syria after WWI; draws borders including diverse ethnic/religious communities.
French leave; series of coups follow.
Military coup; Assad family (Alawite minority) begins 50-year dynasty.
Assad crushes Muslim Brotherhood uprising; 10,000-40,000 civilians killed.
Arab Spring protests met with brutal crackdown; armed uprising; foreign intervention.
Ghouta sarin attack kills 1,400+ civilians; Assad crosses Obama's 'red line'; no military response.
Russian airstrikes save Assad; war enters new phase.
HTS-led rebel offensive captures Damascus in days; Assad flees to Russia after 24 years.

finance.currency_role: Syrian Pound (SYP) — hyperinflation; lost 99% of pre-war value
leverage_tools: Refugee flows as pressure on Europe

resources.oil_gas: Pre-war: modest oil production; now mostly in US/Kurdish-controlled zone
resources.agriculture: Fertile crescent degraded; major pre-war grain producer

follow_the_money.detail: Assad regime industrialised production of Captagon amphetamine; $5B+/yr — largest export
Russia spent $6-10B/yr to maintain Assad; secured Tartus naval base in exchange

theories_disputed.detail: OPCW confirmed Syrian government forces used sarin in multiple attacks.
Russian and Syrian government claim White Helmets staged rescues; Western govts and independent investigators reject this.

connections.detail: Russia kept Assad in power 2015-2024; Tartus naval base
IRGC troops and Hezbollah fought for Assad
Israel struck Iranian positions in Syria hundreds of times
Turkey controls northern Syria; backs anti-Assad rebels
